diff --git a/src/click/core.py b/src/click/core.py
index cc65e89..69a64cd 100644
--- a/src/click/core.py
+++ b/src/click/core.py
@@ -2355,6 +2355,10 @@ class Parameter:
     def process_value(self, ctx: Context, value: t.Any) -> t.Any:
         value = self.type_cast_value(ctx, value)
 
+        # An option is only enforced as required if it was declared with
+        # required=True and, after parsing/defaults/env vars, still ended
+        # up with no usable value (value_is_missing). In that case the CLI
+        # raises MissingParameter to tell the user to supply it.
         if self.required and self.value_is_missing(value):
             raise MissingParameter(ctx=ctx, param=self)

import re
path = "tests/test_basic.py"
s = open(path).read()
old = """    chain(
        ((x, "True") for x in ("1", "true", "t", "yes", "y", "on")),
        ((x, "False") for x in ("0", "false", "f", "no", "n", "off")),
    ),"""

        chain(
            ((x, "True") for x in ("1", "true", "t", "yes", "y", "on")),
            ((x, "False") for x in ("0", "false", "f", "no", "n", "off")),
        )
    ),"""
assert old in s
s = s.replace(old, new)
open(path, "w").write(s)
EOF

index cc65e89..48cbbe7 100644
--- a/src/click/core.py
+++ b/src/click/core.py
@@ -2982,6 +2982,11 @@ class Argument(Parameter):
         required: t.Optional[bool] = None,
         **attrs: t.Any,
     ) -> None:
+        # Rule for whether an argument is required, when not stated
+        # explicitly: an argument that already has a default value can
+        # be omitted, so it isn't required; otherwise, only arguments
+        # that actually consume at least one value (nargs > 0) need to
+        # be required, since a zero-value argument has nothing to supply.
         if required is None:
             if attrs.get("default") is not None:
                 required = False

final_session_calls.command:
cd /work && python - <<'EOF'
import re
path = "tests/test_basic.py"
with open(path) as f:
    content = f.read()

old = '''    chain(
        ((x, "True") for x in ("1", "true", "t", "yes", "y", "on")),
        ((x, "False") for x in ("0", "false", "f", "no", "n", "off")),
    ),'''
new = '''    list(
        chain(
            ((x, "True") for x in ("1", "true", "t", "yes", "y", "on")),
            ((x, "False") for x in ("0", "false", "f", "no", "n", "off")),
        )
    ),'''
assert old in content
content = content.replace(old, new)
with open(path, "w") as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF
